title: Lesson 18 — The Argument

words.definition: to confirm or give support to a statement or theory
words.example: A second witness corroborated her account of the night.

words.definition: to provide evidence to support or prove the truth of
words.example: He could not substantiate his extraordinary claim.

words.definition: to prove a statement or theory to be wrong
words.example: New data refuted the long-standing hypothesis.

words.definition: to argue or prove that a claim or accusation is false
words.example: She rose to rebut the charge point by point.

words.definition: to expose the falseness of a myth, idea, or belief
words.example: The article debunked several popular health myths.

words.definition: based on observation or experiment rather than theory
words.example: Empirical evidence, not opinion, backed the conclusion.

words.definition: able to be defended against attack or objection
words.example: After the new findings, that position was no longer tenable.
A plausible excuse, though not the true one.

words.definition: hesitating or doubting; not to be relied upon; suspect
words.example: A dubious claim propped up by shaky numbers.

words.definition: an opinion or conclusion formed on incomplete information; guesswork
words.example: Without data it was pure conjecture.

words.definition: a statement taken to be true and used as the basis for an argument
words.example: The whole argument rests on a false premise.

words.definition: to deduce or conclude from evidence and reasoning
words.example: We can infer the cause from the pattern in the data.

story: The panel's job was to weigh the evidence. One expert worked to [[corroborate|corroborate]] the theory and [[substantiate|substantiate]] every figure; a rival rose to [[refute|refute]] it, eager to [[rebut|rebut]] each point and [[debunk|debunk]] the shakier claims. The chair demanded [[empirical|empirical]] proof: was the position still [[tenable|tenable]], or merely [[plausible|plausible]]? A [[dubious|dubious]] chart drew groans and was dismissed as pure [[conjecture|conjecture]]. In the end the whole dispute turned on a single shared [[premise|premise]], and from it the judges had to [[infer|infer]] which side was right.
